_meta.purpose: Tarot Major Arcana 22 牌单源数据层（文章生产 + 未来 Tarot 工具复用）
_meta.framework: 模板-Tarot-牌义页框架.md v2（45KB，12模块+6差异化+合规+URL规则+数据层设计）
_meta.crystal_sources: 综合四源（soulfulnwild / astrosofa / labyrinthos / thecrystalcouncil）+ 矿物学 + 五行 + 首饰适配度，逐牌选最优建立 goearthward Crystal Tarot 体系
_meta.cta_validation: 04-内容生产/13.tarot/_qc/_cta-by-slug.json（meaning 16 颗含 Read CTA，其余 OMIT；Shop 按 CATEGORY→SEARCH→HEALING_JEWELRY 三级降级）
_meta.compliance: archetype for self-reflection 框架；逆位用 shadow aspect / invitation to reflect，禁 bad omen / curse / evil；Gentle Note 全站统一
_meta.url_rule: /tarot-{card-slug}-crystals/（避产品词污染，强制 tarot- 前缀 + -crystals 复数后缀）；Hub /crystals-for-tarot-cards/
_meta.module_weights_source: 框架 §2 module_weights 表（22 牌 M2/M3/M7 差异化重心）
_meta.eastern_imagery_added: 2026-06-30 v2 补牌级专属意象词 (Rider-Waite画面+eastern_anchors, 22牌, 用于场景页M7东方节校验)

cards.name: The Fool
cards.theme: New beginnings, leaps of faith, beginner's mind
cards.upright_meaning: A figure steps toward the cliff edge, carrying a white rose (purity of intention) and a small satchel, with a small dog leaping at their heels. The Fool is card zero — the place before the journey begins, where openness has not yet been narrowed by experience. Upright, it speaks to a willingness to begin without needing every step mapped, to trust the beginner's mind that sees possibility rather than risk.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Fool often points to the shadow side of openness — recklessness that skips discernment, or the opposite, hesitation that talks itself out of every beginning. It is not a bad omen; it is an invitation to notice whether a leap before you is wise or impulsive, and to bring a little more care to the edge.
cards.psychological_lens: openness to experience (one of the Big Five traits — the curiosity that seeks novelty and tolerates uncertainty)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: In Tibetan Buddhist practice, the energy of The Fool also resonates with bodhicitta — the beginner's heart, the first arising of aspiration on the path — the willingness to begin before knowing the outcome.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Clear quartz has a long history in East Asian tradition as a stone of clarity, called '菩萨石' (bodhisattva stone) in some old Chinese texts and used for Buddhist prayer beads.

cards.recommended_practice: name one new beginning you are being invited to take, and one practical support you would need before the leap

best_overall.name: Clear Quartz
best_overall.reason: Clear Quartz resonates with The Fool's archetype of unlimited potential and the clarity of the white rose the traveler carries. As the 'Master Healer' that amplifies intention (soulfulnwild's pick for this card), it is a natural companion when you are standing at the edge of your own cliff — a moment that asks for clear sight rather than a reckless leap.

best_upright.name: Green Aventurine
best_upright.reason: Green Aventurine is traditionally tied to opportunity and the willingness to say yes (labyrinthos lists it for The Fool). Hold a piece when you want to greet a fresh start with optimism rather than second-guessing — it pairs well with the card's beginner's-mind energy.

best_reversed.name: Amethyst
best_reversed.reason: When The Fool reverses into recklessness or hesitation, Amethyst offers a steadying, sobering calm (astrosofa's pick). It supports discernment at the edge — helping you tell a wise leap from an impulsive one during shadow work.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens The Fool's love readings, where innocence can tip into naivety. It keeps the heart open while inviting self-respect — a useful anchor before a new relationship begins.

best_daily_wear.name: Moonstone
best_daily_wear.reason: Moonstone is a gentle everyday companion for new beginnings (its traditional association). Worn as jewelry, it serves as a quiet daily reminder to stay open to change without losing your footing.

cards.eastern_lens: 藏传佛教'初发心'对应 / 水晶'菩萨石'古称
cards.source_notes: soulfulnwild=Clear Quartz / astrosofa=Amethyst / labyrinthos=Agate+Aventurine → 我们 best_overall=Clear Quartz(贴 archetype: unlimited potential+clarity，且 Quartz 是首饰百搭主推线)；best_reversed=Amethyst(逆位 reckless 时 grounding+保护)

cards.name: The Magician
cards.theme: Willpower, resourcefulness, 'as above so below'
cards.upright_meaning: A figure stands before a table holding the four suits — cup, sword, wand, pentacle — with one arm raised to the sky and one to the earth, embodying 'as above, so below.' The Magician is the card of having everything you need and knowing how to direct it. Upright, it speaks to resourcefulness: the moment you recognize your tools, skills, and attention are enough to begin shaping something real.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Magician often points to the shadow of resourcefulness — manipulation that bends others to your will, illusion that performs competence without substance, or the quieter form, gifts left untapped. It is an invitation to ask whether you are using your capabilities with integrity or leaking them.
cards.psychological_lens: self-efficacy and agency (the belief that your actions can produce the outcomes you want)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Magician's archetype also appears in Eastern traditions as the creator-deity principle — the recognition that mind and matter shape each other, mirroring the card's 'as above, so below' theme.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Clear quartz, used for Buddhist malas to count mantra recitations, embodies focused intention — the same quality The Magician asks of your tools.

cards.recommended_practice: name one outcome you want to manifest, then list the three real tools or skills you already have to begin it

best_overall.name: Clear Quartz
best_overall.reason: Clear Quartz is the archetypal amplifier — 'as above, so below' in mineral form, directing intention through a single point (astrosofa's pick). For The Magician, it is the master tool that focuses the will; hold it while naming what you intend to build, so the action begins with clarity rather than scattered effort.

best_upright.name: Citrine
best_upright.reason: Citrine carries the warm, active energy of manifestation and confident action (soulfulnwild's pick). When The Magician appears upright, Citrine supports the 'do the work' phase — the follow-through that turns intention into form.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When The Magician tips into illusion or manipulation, Smoky Quartz grounds the will back to honesty and dissolves the trickster energy. It is the shadow-work stone for owning where you have been performative rather than real.

best_love.name: Carnelian
best_love.reason: Carnelian brings warm, courageous presence to The Magician's love readings, where charm can slide into performance. It invites honest attraction rather than manipulation — a stone of confident, genuine relating.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger Eye is a durable everyday stone of willpower and follow-through. Worn as jewelry, it serves as a daily reminder to act on what you say you will — keeping The Magician's resourcefulness in motion.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方创造之神象征 / 水晶念珠专注意图
cards.source_notes: soulfulnwild=Citrine / astrosofa=Clear Quartz / labyrinthos=Agate+Sunstone → 我们 best_overall=Clear Quartz('as above so below'放大器最贴 Magician archetype)；best_upright=Citrine(manifestation 能量)

cards.name: The High Priestess
cards.theme: Inner knowing, the veil between conscious and unconscious, stillness
cards.upright_meaning: A figure sits between two pillars — dark and light — beside a veil embroidered with pomegranates, the moon at her feet. The High Priestess is the keeper of what is known but not said. Upright, she invites stillness so that quieter knowing can surface — the gut sense, the dream residue, the answer that arrives when you stop reasoning for a moment.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The High Priestess often points to a disconnection from that inner knowing — over-talking a decision, ignoring a persistent gut feeling, or hiding behind secrets. It is an invitation to lower the noise and listen again, not a verdict that your intuition is broken.
cards.psychological_lens: interoceptive awareness (the ability to attend to subtle internal signals — heartbeat, gut tension, the felt sense beneath thought)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The High Priestess also resonates with the Tibetan contemplative ideal of stillness — the recognition that insight arises in the space between thoughts, not by chasing it.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Moonstone, sacred in Indian tradition as a moon-connected stone, mirrors the High Priestess's lunar symbolism — the subconscious that waxes and wanes beneath the surface.

cards.recommended_practice: sit in silence for two minutes with a question, and notice the first sensation or image that arrives before you analyze it

best_overall.name: Moonstone
best_overall.reason: Moonstone is the stone of inner knowing and lunar rhythm, a near-universal pick for The High Priestess across crystal traditions. Hold one over your sternum during a quiet sit — it is a tangible anchor for the stillness this card asks, the pause in which quieter answers can surface.

best_upright.name: Selenite
best_upright.reason: Selenite is a high-vibration stone of clarity and the higher mind, often placed near tarot decks to keep the channel clear. For an upright High Priestess draw, it supports the quiet mental space in which intuition speaks — like wiping the mirror before looking.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When intuition is drowned out by noise, Smoky Quartz gently grounds scattered energy back into the body — where interoceptive signals actually live. It is the stone for re-learning to feel the gut sense you may have overruled.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens The High Priestess's love readings, where emotional subtlety can be missed. It invites you to listen to what your heart already knows about a relationship before reaching for more 'data.'

best_daily_wear.name: Labradorite
best_daily_wear.reason: Labradorite, with its flash of hidden color, is a daily wearable reminder of the unconscious depths — a quiet prompt to honor what lies beneath the surface of ordinary days.

cards.eastern_lens: 藏传内观传统 / 印度月亮石(lunar象征)
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Moonstone(贴 High Priestess 的 lunar/intuition archetype)；best_reversed=Smoky Quartz(逆位 noise 时 grounding 回身体)

cards.name: The Empress
cards.theme: Abundance, creativity, fertility, embodied care
cards.upright_meaning: A figure reclines in a lush landscape of wheat and trees, wearing a crown of stars. The Empress is the archetype of embodied abundance — creativity, sensuality, and care that take physical form. Upright, she speaks to a season of growth, of tending something into being (a project, a relationship, a self), and of allowing yourself to receive as well as give.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Empress often points to the shadow of nurturing — smothering care that keeps others small, or its opposite, neglect of your own needs. It is an invitation to ask where giving has become a way to avoid receiving, and where abundance is being blocked by over-functioning.
cards.psychological_lens: nurturance vs over-giving (the relational balance between caring for others and sustaining your own reserves)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Empress archetype resonates with the Earth-mother / Devi tradition in Indian culture — the principle of generative, sustaining abundance that does not deplete itself.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Rose Quartz, the stone of the heart, embodies the Empress's Venus-ruled tenderness — a stone for both giving and receiving love, without which nurturing tips into sacrifice.

cards.recommended_practice: name one thing you have been over-giving, and one small act of receiving you will allow this week

best_overall.name: Rose Quartz
best_overall.reason: Rose Quartz is the Venus-ruled stone of unconditional love — The Empress in mineral form. Hold it over your heart while asking where your care has become one-way; it is the stone for remembering that nurturing includes yourself.

best_upright.name: Green Aventurine
best_upright.reason: Green Aventurine carries the energy of growth, opportunity, and abundance — a natural fit for an upright Empress season. Place it where you tend a creative project to support the patient unfolding of new life.

best_reversed.name: Carnelian
best_reversed.reason: When The Empress reverses into creative block or self-neglect, Carnelian reawakens vitality and the life-force that has gone dormant. It is the stone for putting back into the body what over-giving has drained.

best_love.name: Peridot
best_love.reason: Peridot opens the heart to receiving as well as giving — a useful counterbalance in Empress love readings where one partner carries the emotional labor. (Emerald is the classic Venus stone; Peridot is the accessible, jewelry-friendly alternative.)

best_daily_wear.name: Rhodonite
best_daily_wear.reason: Rhodonite is the stone of compassionate self-worth — a daily reminder that nurturing others begins with honoring your own value. Worn as jewelry, it keeps The Empress's care in balance.

cards.eastern_lens: 印度 Devi 大地母亲 / 东方玉石滋养传统
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Rose Quartz(贴 Empress 的 Venus/nurturing archetype)；Emerald 经典但贵+首饰适配低 → best_love 用 Peridot(Emerald 平价替代)；best_reversed=Carnelian(逆位 creative block 时唤醒 vitality)

cards.name: The Emperor
cards.theme: Structure, authority, foundations, healthy boundaries
cards.upright_meaning: A figure sits on a stone throne adorned with ram heads, holding a scepter, under a barren but stable sky. The Emperor is the archetype of structure — the boundaries, routines, and foundations that allow anything to be built. Upright, he speaks to healthy authority: the discipline that serves a purpose rather than controls for its own sake, and the scaffolding a messy life may be asking for.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Emperor often points to the shadow of structure — rigidity that cannot bend, domination that confuses control with leadership, or its collapse, a structure that has fallen apart. It is an invitation to examine whether your authority is in service of something larger or has hardened into control.
cards.psychological_lens: healthy structure vs control (the difference between boundaries that protect and rules that dominate)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Emperor's stable-throne energy resonates with the Eastern principle of uprightness — the well-ordered foundation on which inner and outer life can stand without collapsing into either chaos or rigidity.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Red Jasper, used across warrior traditions as a stone of endurance, embodies The Emperor's grounded strength — the steady foundation, not the spike of force.

cards.recommended_practice: identify one area of life that lacks structure, and lay a single foundational rule for it this week

best_overall.name: Red Jasper
best_overall.reason: Red Jasper is the stone of steady endurance and grounded foundation — The Emperor's throne in mineral form. Hold it while naming one structure you want to build; its earth-fire stability supports the patient discipline this card asks, without the spike of domination.

best_upright.name: Carnelian
best_upright.reason: Carnelian brings the active, courageous energy of healthy leadership to an upright Emperor draw — the confidence to set a structure and stand by it. It supports the Aries-ruled initiative this card carries.

best_reversed.name: Sodalite
best_reversed.reason: When The Emperor reverses into rigidity or domination, Sodalite cools the overheated will and brings logic back into authority. It is the stone for loosening control without losing structure — inviting reflection over reaction.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens The Emperor's love readings, where authority can tip into control. It invites structure that protects the relationship rather than manages the partner.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger Eye is a daily stone of integrity and grounded willpower — a wearable reminder that real authority is consistent, not forceful. It keeps The Emperor's structure in check through the workday.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'正'稳固原则 / 战士传统 Red Jasper
cards.source_notes: 五行裁决=Red Jasper(火土属性稳固最贴 Emperor 结构牌)；best_reversed=Sodalite(逆位 rigidity 时降温+logic)

cards.name: The Hierophant
cards.theme: Tradition, lineage, wisdom, learned structure
cards.upright_meaning: A figure in religious vestments sits between two pillars, blessing two initiates who kneel before him. The Hierophant is the archetype of received wisdom — knowledge passed down through lineage, tradition, and teaching. Upright, he speaks to the value of learning from those who came before, of standing on a structured foundation rather than inventing everything alone.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Hierophant often points to the shadow of tradition — dogma clung to past its usefulness, or rebellion against any structure without examining which still serves. It is an invitation to ask which inherited beliefs are still true for you and which have become empty rules.
cards.psychological_lens: conformity vs autonomy (the developmental tension between inherited frameworks and self-authored values)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Hierophant resonates deeply with the Tibetan lineage (lineage transmission) principle — the unbroken chain of teaching from teacher to student that carries wisdom forward without diluting it.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Amethyst, a stone long associated with spiritual devotion across both Western and Eastern traditions, embodies The Hierophant's reverence for the sacred and the taught.

cards.recommended_practice: name one inherited belief you have never examined, and ask whether it still matches what you actually know

best_overall.name: Amethyst
best_overall.reason: Amethyst is the stone of spiritual wisdom and devotion — The Hierophant's vestments in mineral form. Hold it while studying or sitting with a teacher; it supports the reverent attention this card asks, the openness to receive rather than reinvent.

best_upright.name: Sodalite
best_upright.reason: Sodalite supports the mental discipline of genuine learning — the willingness to follow a structured teaching before critiquing it. For an upright Hierophant draw, it aids the humble study that tradition asks.

best_reversed.name: Carnelian
best_reversed.reason: When The Hierophant reverses into dogma or stale belief, Carnelian reawakens the courage to question and the vitality of self-authored conviction. It is the stone for releasing a rule that no longer fits.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz brings warmth to The Hierophant's love readings, where convention can overshadow feeling. It invites the heart's truth to have equal weight with what is 'supposed to' happen in a relationship.

best_daily_wear.name: Lapis Lazuli
best_daily_wear.reason: Lapis Lazuli has been the stone of wisdom, truth, and royalty across Persian, Egyptian, and Buddhist art for millennia. Worn daily, it is a reminder to seek truth through both lineage and your own discernment.

cards.eastern_lens: 藏传 lineage 传承 / Lapis Lazuli 跨波斯-埃及-佛教智慧石
cards.source_notes: labyrinthos+四源共识=Amethyst(贴 Hierophant 的 spiritual wisdom archetype)；best_reversed=Carnelian(逆位 dogma 时唤醒质疑勇气)

cards.name: The Lovers
cards.theme: Union, values-aligned choice, partnership
cards.upright_meaning: A man and woman stand beneath an angel with outspread wings, the sun behind it, with a tree of fruit and a tree of knowledge behind each figure. The Lovers is not only romantic — it is the card of values-aligned choice, the kind of union (with a person, a path, a project) that requires you to know what you stand for first. Upright, it speaks to a choice made from alignment rather than pressure.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Lovers often points to misalignment — a choice that does not match your values, disharmony in a partnership, or avoidance of a decision that needs making. It is an invitation to look honestly at where you and another (or you and a path) have stopped rowing in the same direction.
cards.psychological_lens: attachment and values (the link between what we are drawn toward and what we actually believe in)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Lovers' theme of conscious union resonates with the Eastern ideal of 'right association' — choosing companions and paths that support your stated values rather than fracture them.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Rose Quartz, the universal stone of the heart across cultures, anchors The Lovers' invitation to choose from love rather than fear or convenience.

cards.recommended_practice: name a choice in front of you, and write the value each option would honor or violate

best_overall.name: Rhodonite
best_overall.reason: Rhodonite is the stone of compassionate relationship and emotional healing — a deeper choice than rose quartz for The Lovers because it addresses the wounds partnerships surface. Hold it while weighing a values-aligned choice; it supports clear, self-respecting love.

best_upright.name: Rose Quartz
best_upright.reason: Rose Quartz is the universal heart stone for harmonious partnership. For an upright Lovers draw, it supports the open, undefended love that values-aligned union requires.

best_reversed.name: Rose Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When The Lovers reverses into disharmony, Rose Quartz is the gentle stone for reopening the heart and softening resentment. It is the shadow-work stone for repairing trust without bypassing honesty.

best_love.name: Rhodochrosite
best_love.reason: Rhodochrosite is the stone of self-love and the inner child — useful in Lovers love readings where old patterns replay. It supports relationship healing that begins with how you treat yourself.

best_daily_wear.name: Malachite
best_daily_wear.reason: Malachite is a stone of transformation in relationship — it surfaces what needs to shift and protects the heart through it. Worn daily, it is a reminder that conscious partnership asks for ongoing growth.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'right association'择友择道 / Rose Quartz 跨文化心轮石
cards.source_notes: Lovers/Empress 用 rose-quartz 作 best_overall 时 best_love 换 rhodonite/rhodochrosite 错开(框架§5防同质化)；best_reversed=Rose Quartz(逆位 disharmony 时修复)

cards.name: The Chariot
cards.theme: Will, direction, triumph through focused effort
cards.upright_meaning: A figure drives a chariot pulled by two sphinxes — one dark, one light — that pull in opposite directions, yet are guided forward. The Chariot is the archetype of focused will: the triumph that comes not from raw power but from harnessing opposing forces toward a single aim. Upright, it speaks to direction, to the moment scattered energy aligns into momentum.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Chariot often points to force without direction — aggression that scatters rather than advances, two impulses pulling you apart, or momentum stalled. It is an invitation to ask what you are actually driving toward before pressing harder on the gas.
cards.psychological_lens: conscientiousness and drive (the capacity to organize competing impulses toward a goal)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Chariot's theme of harnessing opposing forces resonates with the Eastern principle of the middle way — strength that comes from integration, not from suppressing one side.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Hematite, used in many traditions as a grounding stone, embodies The Chariot's rooted, focused will — the kind of steadiness that holds opposing forces in line.

cards.recommended_practice: name the two competing pulls on your time, and the single goal that could harness both

best_overall.name: Hematite
best_overall.reason: Hematite is the stone of grounded willpower and focus — The Chariot's steady hand on the reins in mineral form. Hold it while naming your single direction; its weight and stability support the concentration this card asks, the unification of opposing pulls.

best_upright.name: Carnelian
best_upright.reason: Carnelian fuels the active, driven energy of forward motion (labyrinthos lists Onyx and Carnelian for The Chariot). For an upright draw, it supports the courage and stamina of sustained pursuit.

best_reversed.name: Carnelian
best_reversed.reason: When The Chariot reverses into aggression or scattered force, Carnelian (used mindfully) can re-channel raw drive into directed action — the same energy, harnessed rather than spent.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens The Chariot's love readings, where the drive to 'win' the relationship can overpower tenderness. It invites shared momentum rather than one partner dragging the other.

best_daily_wear.name: Tiger Eye
best_daily_wear.reason: Tiger Eye is a daily stone of focused willpower and integrity. Worn as jewelry, it keeps The Chariot's direction in view through the workday — a reminder of what you committed to move toward.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'中道'整合对立力 / Hematite 扎根意志
cards.source_notes: labyrinthos=Onyx+Carnelian → 我们 best_overall=Hematite(grounded willpower 最贴 Chariot 的'harness opposing forces'archetype，首饰适配优于 Onyx)；best_upright/best_reversed=Carnelian

cards.name: Strength
cards.theme: Courage through softness, patience, inner fortitude
cards.upright_meaning: A woman calmly opens the jaws of a lion with bare hands, often with an infinity symbol above her head, flowers forming a wreath in her hair and around the lion's neck. Strength is the archetype of power through gentleness — courage that tames not by force but by patient presence. Upright, it speaks to mastering the raw instinct within (fear, anger, appetite) through compassion rather than suppression.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, Strength often points to the shadow of inner fortitude — self-doubt that underestimates your capacity, raw impulse overriding hard-won mastery, or the burnout of pretending to be strong. It is an invitation to ask where you have stopped being gentle with yourself in the name of being tough.
cards.psychological_lens: emotional regulation (the skill of meeting intense feeling with awareness rather than suppression or explosion)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: Strength's 'gentle taming of the lion' resonates with the Eastern contemplative principle of taming the mind through non-violent attention — strength that does not wage war on itself.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Tiger Eye, used across traditions as a stone of courage and integrity, embodies Strength's measured power — brave without being brutal.

cards.recommended_practice: name one raw impulse you usually suppress or vent, and meet it today with one minute of patient attention instead

best_overall.name: Tiger Eye
best_overall.reason: Tiger Eye is the stone of balanced courage — brave without aggression, the gentle power this card names. Hold it when you need to meet fear or anger with presence rather than force; it anchors the patient mastery Strength depicts.

best_upright.name: Sunstone
best_upright.reason: Sunstone carries the warm, Leo-ruled radiance of healthy self-worth. For an upright Strength draw, it supports the confidence that lets you face what is hard without flinching.

best_reversed.name: Citrine
best_reversed.reason: When Strength reverses into self-doubt or burnout, Citrine restores the solar, confident energy that has dimmed. It is the stone for remembering your own capability when self-worth has dipped.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz brings compassion to Strength's love readings, where the instinct to 'manage' a partner can override tenderness. It invites patience with the other's raw edges — and your own.

best_daily_wear.name: Carnelian
best_daily_wear.reason: Carnelian is a daily stone of courage and grounded confidence. Worn as jewelry, it keeps Strength's gentle power within reach through the workday — a quiet reminder of inner fortitude.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'调伏'taming the mind 非暴力 / Tiger Eye 平衡勇气
cards.source_notes: labyrinthos=Sunstone → 我们 best_overall=Tiger Eye(平衡勇气最贴 Strength 的'gentle power'archetype，首饰百搭)；best_reversed=Citrine(逆位 self-doubt 时恢复 solar confidence)

cards.name: The Hermit
cards.theme: Solitude, inner guidance, the lantern of self-knowledge
cards.upright_meaning: An old figure in a hooded cloak stands on a snowy peak, holding a lantern that holds a six-pointed star, a staff in his other hand. The Hermit is the archetype of solitude in service of inner knowing — withdrawing not to escape but to hear what the crowd drowns out. Upright, it speaks to the value of turning inward, of carrying your own lantern rather than borrowing others' light.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Hermit often points to the shadow of solitude — isolation that has become loneliness, withdrawal that cuts you off from the sharing your wisdom needs, or stuckness disguised as retreat. It is an invitation to ask whether your solitude is feeding you or hiding you.
cards.psychological_lens: solitude vs isolation (the difference between chosen time alone that restores and enforced alone-time that depletes)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Hermit resonates deeply with the Tibetan practice of retreat — structured solitude undertaken for insight, not escape, with a defined end and a return to share what was learned.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Labradorite, with its hidden flash revealed only at certain angles, embodies The Hermit's theme of inner light — the guidance that does not need to be displayed to be real.

cards.recommended_practice: schedule 30 minutes of phone-free solitude this week, with one question to sit with — and notice if it restores or depletes you

best_overall.name: Labradorite
best_overall.reason: Labradorite is the stone of inner light and transformation — The Hermit's lantern in mineral form, its flash hidden until the angle is right. Hold it during solitude; it is a tangible reminder that the guidance you seek is already within, revealed by patient attention.

best_upright.name: Amethyst
best_upright.reason: Amethyst supports the contemplative depth and spiritual clarity of true retreat. For an upright Hermit draw, it aids the inward listening this card asks — the kind of solitude that returns you to yourself.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When The Hermit reverses into isolation or withdrawal, Smoky Quartz gently grounds you back into the body and the present — the antidote to the dissociation that disguises hiding as retreat.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens The Hermit's love readings, where a need for space can read as distance. It supports communicating your solitude as restoration, not rejection, so connection survives the withdrawal.

best_daily_wear.name: Lapis Lazuli
best_daily_wear.reason: Lapis Lazuli is a stone of deep inner wisdom carried across cultures. Worn daily, it keeps The Hermit's inquiry alive in ordinary settings — a reminder to seek your own truth, not the crowd's.

cards.eastern_lens: 藏传'闭关'retreat 传统 / Labradorite 内在之光
cards.source_notes: labyrinthos=Amethyst/Iolite → 我们 best_overall=Labradorite(inner light 最贴 Hermit 的'lantern'archetype，首饰适配)；best_reversed=Smoky Quartz(逆位 isolation 时 grounding)

cards.name: Wheel of Fortune
cards.theme: Cycles, change, the turning of fortune, momentum
cards.upright_meaning: A great wheel turns, surrounded by winged figures — a sphinx above, a snake descending, a jackal-headed figure rising — with the four fixed signs of the zodiac in the corners reading books. The Wheel of Fortune is the archetype of cycles — the recognition that nothing stays the same, that what is up will turn and what is down will rise. Upright, it speaks to momentum, to a turning point, and to the wisdom of riding change rather than resisting it.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Wheel often points to resistance to change — holding on to a phase that has ended, or a streak where momentum feels against you. It is not a verdict of bad luck; it is an invitation to ask what you are refusing to release, and what is actually within your influence versus what is not.
cards.psychological_lens: locus of control (the distinction between what you can shape and what you must accept as outside your control)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Wheel resonates with the Eastern recognition of impermanence and cyclic existence — not as fatalism, but as the freedom that comes from no longer clinging to a single fixed outcome.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Turquoise, carried for protection across the Silk Road's turning fortunes, embodies the traveler's steady heart through change — fortune's companion, not its victim.

cards.recommended_practice: sort one current situation into 'within my influence' and 'outside my control,' and act only on the first column

best_overall.name: Green Aventurine
best_overall.reason: Green Aventurine is the stone of opportunity and the willingness to move with change. The Wheel turns whether you resist or not — hold Aventurine when you need to meet a turning point with openness rather than grip, trusting your own response within the cycle.

best_upright.name: Citrine
best_upright.reason: Citrine carries the abundant, Jupiter-ruled energy of fortune's upturn. For an upright Wheel draw, it supports optimism and the readiness to act when momentum is with you.

best_reversed.name: Obsidian
best_reversed.reason: When The Wheel reverses into resistance or stuckness, Obsidian helps surface the hidden pattern you are clinging to. It is the stone for honest shadow-work around what you refuse to release.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz steadies The Wheel's love readings, where the phase of a relationship may be turning. It supports meeting change in partnership with heart, not panic.

best_daily_wear.name: Turquoise
best_daily_wear.reason: Turquoise has been carried for protection and good fortune across cultures for millennia. Worn daily, it is a steady companion through fortune's ups and downs — a reminder that you are the traveler, not the wheel.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方无常/轮回观非宿命 / Turquoise 丝路护身传统
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Aventurine(opportunity 最贴 Wheel 的'cycles/turning'archetype)；best_reversed=Obsidian(逆位 resistance 时 shadow work)

cards.name: Justice
cards.theme: Fairness, accountability, truth, balance
cards.upright_meaning: A figure sits on a throne between two pillars, holding a sword upright in one hand and a set of scales in the other, a veil behind. Justice is the archetype of truth and accountability — not punishment, but the honest weighing of what is. Upright, it speaks to fairness (toward yourself as well as others), to a decision that needs to be made on the evidence rather than on wish or fear.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, Justice often points to imbalance — a situation that is not fair, dishonesty (your own or another's), or avoidance of responsibility for what you have done. It is an invitation to restore honesty where it has slipped, starting with your own account of things.
cards.psychological_lens: justice sensitivity (the human responsiveness to fairness, both as a value and as a felt sense when things are not right)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: Justice resonates with the Eastern principle of karma understood as cause-and-effect — not fatalistic punishment, but the honest recognition that actions have consequences that can be examined and learned from.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Lapis Lazuli, associated with truth and just rule across Persian, Egyptian, and Buddhist art, embodies Justice's demand for what is honest and right.

cards.recommended_practice: write down one situation as factually as you can, separating what you know from what you assume or fear

best_overall.name: Lapis Lazuli
best_overall.reason: Lapis Lazuli is the stone of truth, wisdom, and just discernment — Justice's scales in mineral form. Hold it when you need to weigh a situation honestly; its deep blue supports seeing clearly, without flinching from what is true.

best_upright.name: Sodalite
best_upright.reason: Sodalite supports the rational clarity and logic a fair weighing requires. For an upright Justice draw, it aids sorting fact from feeling — the honest evidence this card asks you to gather.

best_reversed.name: Carnelian
best_reversed.reason: When Justice reverses into dishonesty or avoidance, Carnelian reawakens the courage to take responsibility and speak honestly. It is the stone for facing what you have been sidestepping.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens Justice's love readings, where fairness can read as coldness. It supports honest accountability in partnership without losing the warmth that makes repair possible.

best_daily_wear.name: Hematite
best_daily_wear.reason: Hematite is a daily stone of grounded clarity and boundary. Worn as jewelry, it keeps Justice's honesty within reach — a reminder to weigh fairly and speak what is true through ordinary days.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方因果(cause-effect非宿命)/ Lapis Lazuli 真理之石
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Lapis(truth/wisdom 最贴 Justice archetype)；best_reversed=Carnelian(逆位 dishonesty 时勇气担责)

cards.name: The Hanged Man
cards.theme: Perspective shift, surrender, willing pause
cards.upright_meaning: A figure hangs upside down by one ankle from a T-shaped tree, face serene, a halo of light around the head. The Hanged Man is the archetype of willing surrender — the pause that changes perspective, the letting go that is chosen rather than forced. Upright, it speaks to seeing something from a new angle, to the insight that arrives only when you stop pushing.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Hanged Man often points to the shadow of surrender — stalling disguised as patience, martyrdom that wears sacrifice as identity, or a refusal to let go of a perspective that no longer serves. It is an invitation to ask whether your pause is serving insight or hiding indecision.
cards.psychological_lens: cognitive reappraisal (the skill of deliberately shifting the frame through which you see a situation)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Hanged Man's inversion resonates with the Eastern contemplative practice of 'reversing habitual view' — loosening the mind's fixed perspective to see what was always there but unseen.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Selenite, a stone of mental clarity and higher perspective, supports the cleansed mirror this card asks — the clean view that comes only after the old angle is released.

cards.recommended_practice: take one fixed belief, and argue the opposite side honestly for three minutes

best_overall.name: Selenite
best_overall.reason: Selenite is the stone of clarity and higher perspective — The Hanged Man's halo in mineral form. Hold it overhead during a deliberate pause; it supports the cleansed mental space from which a new angle on an old situation can finally appear.

best_upright.name: Amethyst
best_upright.reason: Amethyst supports the contemplative depth of a chosen surrender. For an upright Hanged Man draw, it aids the stillness in which a fixed perspective can loosen — the patience this card asks before the insight arrives.

best_reversed.name: Bloodstone
best_reversed.reason: When The Hanged Man reverses into stalling or martyrdom, Bloodstone grounds the floating energy back into the body and restores the courage to act once the insight has come. It is the stone for ending the pause when it has done its work.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens The Hanged Man's love readings, where a needed pause can read as withdrawal. It supports surrender in partnership as chosen space, not avoidance.

best_daily_wear.name: Aquamarine
best_daily_wear.reason: Aquamarine is a stone of calm courage and clear communication. Worn daily, it is a gentle reminder to stay fluid — to release fixed positions and meet the day with a more open frame.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'反转惯常视角'内观 / Selenite 高维清明
cards.source_notes: labyrinthos=Ametrine(数据库无，拆 Amethyst+Selenite)→ 我们 best_overall=Selenite(higher perspective 最贴 Hanged Man 的'halo/反转视角'archetype)；best_upright=Amethyst(拆解)；best_reversed=Bloodstone(逆位 stalling 时 grounding 回行动)

cards.name: Death
cards.theme: Endings, transformation, the clearing that makes space for rebirth
cards.upright_meaning: A skeleton in armor rides a pale horse, the sun rising between two towers in the distance, a fallen king before it, a bishop pleading, a child watching with curiosity. Death is almost never literal — it is the archetype of necessary ending, the transformation that clears what has completed so something new can live. Upright, it speaks to release, to the doorway that opens only when you let an old form of life close.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, Death often points to resistance to that ending — clinging to a phase, relationship, or identity that has already completed, which leads to a kind of stagnation or slow decay. It is not a curse; it is an invitation to consider what you are refusing to let end, and what is being kept alive only by force.
cards.psychological_lens: meaning-making after loss (the capacity to find coherent narrative in significant change or ending)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: Death resonates with the Eastern contemplation of impermanence — not as morbidity, but as the clarity that frees you to live fully because nothing is held as permanent.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Obsidian, used in Mesoamerican and Eastern traditions for truth-telling and release, embodies Death's unflinching honesty about what must end.

cards.recommended_practice: name one thing in your life that has already ended but you have not yet let yourself acknowledge

best_overall.name: Obsidian
best_overall.reason: Obsidian is the stone of truth, release, and shadow work — Death's unflinching mirror. Hold it when you need to face an ending honestly; it supports the clearing this card asks, the release of what has already completed so that life can continue.

best_upright.name: Malachite
best_upright.reason: Malachite is the stone of deep transformation and the surfacing of what is ready to shift. For an upright Death draw, it supports the sometimes uncomfortable process of change that precedes rebirth.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When Death reverses into resistance or stagnation, Smoky Quartz gently helps release the old energy that is being clutched past its time. It is the stone for loosening the grip on what has already ended.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz brings tenderness to Death's love readings, where a relationship may be transforming or completing. It supports grieving what is ending with heart, rather than forcing it to stay the same.

best_daily_wear.name: Garnet
best_daily_wear.reason: Garnet is a stone of regeneration and commitment that survives change. Worn daily, it is a quiet reminder that what is true endures transformation — that endings and continuity can coexist.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'无常'观(非病态)/ Obsidian 诚实之镜
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Obsidian(truth/release 最贴 Death archetype)；best_reversed=Smoky Quartz(逆位 resistance 时 release 旧能量)；合规：明示'almost never literal'+ 逆位非 curse

cards.name: Temperance
cards.theme: Balance, blending, patience, the middle way
cards.upright_meaning: A winged figure stands with one foot in water and one on land, pouring liquid between two cups in a continuous, unhurried flow, a path leading to a crown of light between sun and mountains. Temperance is the archetype of balanced blending — the patient art of holding opposites without forcing either side. Upright, it speaks to the middle way, to the slow, steady mixing that produces something neither extreme could alone.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, Temperance often points to imbalance — excess in one direction, impatience with the slow pace of true integration, or a swing between extremes. It is an invitation to ask where you have lost the middle, and what patient blending a situation is asking for.
cards.psychological_lens: self-regulation (the capacity to hold opposing states or needs without collapsing into either extreme)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: Temperance resonates directly with the Eastern principle of the middle way — not the mediocre average, but the dynamic balance that holds opposites in living tension.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Amethyst, historically associated with sobriety and balanced mind (the Greek root 'amethystos' means 'not intoxicated'), embodies Temperance's measured clarity.

cards.recommended_practice: name two opposing needs pulling at you this week, and one small way to honor both rather than choose

best_overall.name: Amethyst
best_overall.reason: Amethyst is the stone of balanced mind and measured calm (the very name means 'not intoxicated'). Hold it between the two cups of any decision — a work-rest tension, a head-heart split; it supports the unhurried blending Temperance depicts.

best_upright.name: Fluorite
best_upright.reason: Fluorite is the stone of focus and orderly mental flow. For an upright Temperance draw, it supports the patient integration of different inputs into a coherent whole — the slow mixing this card honors.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When Temperance reverses into excess or impatience, Smoky Quartz grounds the overcharged system back to a steady baseline. It is the stone for settling the swing between extremes.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz brings balance to Temperance's love readings, where two people must blend different needs. It supports the patient mixing of hearts that sustainable partnership requires.

best_daily_wear.name: Amazonite
best_daily_wear.reason: Amazonite is a daily stone of calm, honest balance and harmonious communication. Worn as jewelry, it keeps Temperance's measured tone within reach — a reminder to hold the middle through ordinary frictions.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'中道'(动态平衡非折中)/ Amethyst 古意'不醉'平衡心
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Amethyst(平衡心/不醉 最贴 Temperance 的'middle way'archetype)；best_reversed=Smoky Quartz(逆位 excess 时 grounding)

cards.name: The Devil
cards.theme: Bondage, attachment, shadow awareness — and the liberation that comes from seeing it
cards.upright_meaning: A horned figure perches on a dark pillar, two figures chained loosely before it — but the chains are loose enough to lift off, their horns lit, a reversed pentagram above. The Devil is the archetype of attachment and shadow — not evil, but the patterns, addictions, and self-limiting stories we are bound by. Crucially, the chains are loose: upright, it speaks to seeing where you are caught, and recognizing the bond is more loosable than it feels.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Devil often points to the beginning of liberation — the moment you see through an attachment, break a pattern, or reclaim what was projected onto an outer 'captor.' It is not a worsening; it is the shadow-work paying off, the chains being tested for how loose they actually are.
cards.psychological_lens: shadow integration (the process of recognizing and reclaiming the disowned parts of the self that drive compulsive patterns)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Devil resonates with the Eastern contemplative practice of facing 'inner demons' (the personified defilements) directly — not as literal entities, but as the named forms of attachment and aversion that lose their grip when seen clearly.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Black Tourmaline, used across traditions for protection and grounding, embodies the steady, unflinching presence needed to look at shadow without being swallowed by it.

cards.recommended_practice: name one attachment or pattern you are caught in, and notice — without acting yet — where the chain might already be loose

best_overall.name: Black Tourmaline
best_overall.reason: Black Tourmaline is the stone of protection, grounding, and energetic boundaries — the steady presence needed to look at shadow without being swallowed. Hold it while naming an attachment honestly; it supports seeing the chain clearly, which is the first step to lifting it.

best_upright.name: Smoky Quartz
best_upright.reason: Smoky Quartz gently transmutes the dense, stuck energy of attachment. For an upright Devil draw, it supports the slow release work that loosens the chain — the grounding that makes shadow visible without overwhelm.

best_reversed.name: Clear Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When The Devil reverses into liberation, Clear Quartz clears the residue of the pattern just released and clarifies what you want to build in its place. It is the stone for the clean slate that follows a loosened chain.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens The Devil's love readings, where attachment can masquerade as love. It supports the honest heart-work of telling devotion from compulsion.

best_daily_wear.name: Black Onyx
best_daily_wear.reason: Black Onyx is a daily stone of self-mastery and steady strength. Worn as jewelry, it keeps The Devil's shadow-awareness grounded through ordinary temptations — a reminder of your own agency.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'对治内魔'(非实体)/ Black Tourmaline 守护扎根
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Black Tourmaline(保护/边界 最贴 Devil 的'shadow-awareness'archetype)；合规：明示'chains are loose'+ 非evil/curse；best_reversed=Clear Quartz(逆位 liberation 时清理)

cards.name: The Tower
cards.theme: Sudden revelation, structural collapse, the truth that breaks what was unstable
cards.upright_meaning: A tall tower struck by lightning, two figures falling, a crown knocked from its top, flames and dark sky. The Tower is the archetype of sudden, often unsettling revelation — the collapse of a structure (a belief, a plan, a situation) that was already unstable. Upright, it speaks to the truth that arrives in a flash, and to the possibility of rebuilding on foundations that are now actually true.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Tower often points to avoiding the lesson — propping up a structure that has already shown its instability, or resisting a change that is necessary. It is not a worsening of disaster; it is an invitation to stop forestalling what is trying to happen, and to meet the rebuild rather than delay it.
cards.psychological_lens: post-traumatic growth (the way destabilizing events can, over time, clarify values and reorganize life around what is actually true)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Tower resonates with the Eastern recognition that the structures we build are impermanent — and that their collapse is not punishment but clarity, the false falling away to reveal what was always underneath.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Smoky Quartz, used for grounding through upheaval, embodies The Tower's call to land in the body when the familiar structure falls away.

cards.recommended_practice: name one structure in your life that has already shown cracks, and one true thing you would rebuild on if it fell

best_overall.name: Smoky Quartz
best_overall.reason: Smoky Quartz is the stone of grounding through upheaval and gentle transformation (soulfulnwild's pick for The Tower). Hold it when the familiar structure falls; it keeps you present and rooted while the dust settles — the steady companion for rebuilding on truer foundations. (astrosofa suggests Moldavite for rapid change; we choose Smoky Quartz for jewelry-fit, accessibility, and the same grounding-transformation quality.)
best_overall.source: soulfulnwild + 首饰适配裁决(astrosofa Moldavite 贵+小众降级)

best_upright.name: Labradorite
best_upright.reason: Labradorite is the stone of transformation and the flash of hidden truth — fitting for the sudden revelation The Tower brings. For an upright draw, it supports integrating the change once the dust settles.

best_reversed.name: Black Tourmaline
best_reversed.reason: When The Tower reverses into resistance or avoided lessons, Black Tourmaline offers protection and grounding for the harder work of meeting what you have been postponing. It is the stone for facing the rebuild you have been delaying.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens The Tower's love readings, where a sudden truth may shake a relationship. It supports staying tender through the disclosure, so what is rebuilt can be honest rather than defended.

best_daily_wear.name: Hematite
best_daily_wear.reason: Hematite is a daily stone of grounded stability — vital during a Tower season. Worn as jewelry, it keeps you rooted through ordinary shakes, a steady reminder that you can stand even when structures move.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'无常/结构性崩塌即清明'/ Smoky Quartz 动荡中扎根
cards.source_notes: astrosofa=Moldavite(rapid transformation，但贵+小众不适合首饰站)/ soulfulnwild=Smoky Quartz → 我们 best_overall=Smoky Quartz(grounding+transformation，首饰适配+平价+同属性)，Moldavite 标注为 astrosofa 推荐的 alt mention；best_reversed=Black Tourmaline(防护+吸收负能量)；合规：明示'already unstable'+ 逆位非 worsening

cards.name: The Star
cards.theme: Hope, renewal, guidance after the storm
cards.upright_meaning: A figure kneels at the edge of water, pouring water from two vessels — one into the pool, one onto the land — a great eight-pointed star above surrounded by smaller stars, a bird in a tree nearby. The Star is the archetype of hope after the storm — the calm that follows The Tower, the guidance that returns. Upright, it speaks to renewal, to reconnecting with what gives you meaning, and to the quiet faith that the way forward is being shown.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Star often points to a temporary disconnection from hope — feeling adrift, faith worn thin by difficulty, the guidance obscured by discouragement. It is not a verdict of despair; it is an invitation to ask what has dimmed your sense of meaning, and to let even a small light back in.
cards.psychological_lens: hope theory (the capacity to identify pathways toward what you want and the agency to begin moving — hope as a skill, not just a feeling)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Star resonates with the Eastern ideal of bodhicitta, the awakened heart-mind — the steady, luminous aspiration that re-arises even after difficulty, not as denial but as renewed direction.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Aquamarine, the stone of calm courage and clear water, embodies The Star's serene renewal — the still pool this card depicts.

cards.recommended_practice: name one thing that has renewed even a little hope recently, however small, and one next step toward it

best_overall.name: Aquamarine
best_overall.reason: Aquamarine is the stone of calm courage and clear renewal — The Star's still pool in mineral form. Hold it when you need to reconnect with hope after a hard season; its cool, clear energy supports the serene faith this card offers, the quiet trust that the way is being shown.

best_upright.name: Selenite
best_upright.reason: Selenite is the stone of clarity and cleansing light. For an upright Star draw, it supports the cleared, lifted state of mind in which guidance can be perceived again — the bright sky after the storm.

best_reversed.name: Lepidolite
best_reversed.reason: When The Star reverses into despair or thin faith, Lepidolite (naturally containing calming lithium) gently soothes the discouraged nervous system. It is the stone for the tender work of letting hope back in, without forcing it.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz brings warmth to The Star's love readings, where a relationship may be renewing after difficulty. It supports reopening the heart in trust, gently, after it has been through a storm.

best_daily_wear.name: Moonstone
best_daily_wear.reason: Moonstone is a daily stone of new beginnings and inner light. Worn as jewelry, it keeps The Star's hope within reach — a soft reminder that guidance returns, even on ordinary cloudy days.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'菩提心'觉醒心(非否认)/ Aquamarine 清明之水
cards.source_notes: labyrinthos=Aquamarine → 我们 best_overall=Aquamarine(serene renewal 最贴 Star 的'still pool after storm'archetype)；best_reversed=Lepidolite(逆位 despair 时 soothing)

cards.name: The Moon
cards.theme: Intuition, the subconscious, illusion, what is hidden coming to light
cards.upright_meaning: A full moon between two towers, a crayfish emerging from a pool, a dog and a wolf howling on a path that winds between them into distant mountains. The Moon is the archetype of the subconscious — intuition, dreams, and also illusion, the things half-seen. Upright, it speaks to a time when not everything is clear, when the rational mind cannot fully map what is surfacing, and the work is to stay with the uncertainty rather than force premature clarity.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Moon often points to the surfacing of what was hidden — a deception coming to light, confusion beginning to clear, fear losing its grip. It is not a worsening; it is often a release, the moment what was murky begins to resolve into understanding.
cards.psychological_lens: unconscious processing (the way dreams, symptoms, and half-formed impressions carry information that the conscious mind has not yet organized)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Moon resonates with the Eastern contemplative recognition of 'the unconscious depths' — the layers of mind beneath ordinary awareness, approached through dream practice and quiet observation rather than force.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Moonstone, named for the moon itself across Indian and Roman traditions, embodies The Moon's lunar, intuitive quality — the inner light that is reflected rather than direct.

cards.recommended_practice: spend two minutes with an unclear feeling, and write the images that arise rather than trying to explain them

best_overall.name: Moonstone
best_overall.reason: Moonstone is the lunar stone of intuition and the subconscious — The Moon in mineral form, its light reflected rather than direct. Hold it during uncertain, half-lit seasons; it supports staying with the not-yet-clear without forcing premature answers, the patience this card asks.

best_upright.name: Selenite
best_upright.reason: Selenite is a stone of mental clarity and the subtle light that pierces illusion. For an upright Moon draw, it supports the gentle illumination that lets hidden things be seen at the right pace — neither rushed nor avoided.

best_reversed.name: Labradorite
best_reversed.reason: When The Moon reverses and what was hidden begins to surface, Labradorite supports the transformation and the clear seeing that integration requires. It is the stone for the moment confusion releases into understanding.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz steadies The Moon's love readings, where not everything may be clear yet. It supports staying open-hearted through the uncertainty rather than building defenses against what has not been confirmed.

best_daily_wear.name: Amethyst
best_daily_wear.reason: Amethyst is a daily stone of calm intuition and spiritual clarity. Worn as jewelry, it keeps The Moon's receptive awareness available through ordinary days — a reminder to honor the not-yet-spoken.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'无意识深处'/梦观 / Moonstone 月光内映之石
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Moonstone(lunar/intuition 最贴 Moon archetype)；best_reversed=Labradorite(逆位 confusion releasing 时 clear seeing)；合规：明示'illusion/uncertainty'+ 逆位非 worsening 是 release

cards.name: The Sun
cards.theme: Joy, vitality, clarity, success
cards.upright_meaning: A bright sun with a content child riding a white horse beneath it, sunflowers turning toward the light, a wall behind. The Sun is the archetype of joyful clarity — vitality, optimism, the simple good of being alive. Upright, it speaks to a season of warmth and success, and also to the cultivation of joy — the reminder that even good things are noticed and tended, not assumed.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The Sun often points to a temporary dimming — muted joy, a setback after a high, or the shadow of too much sun, ego inflation and excessive optimism. It is not a forecast of failure; it is an invitation to ask where you have stopped tending the joy that was once vivid, or where confidence has crossed into overreach.
cards.psychological_lens: positive affect (the role of cultivated joy and vitality in resilience and well-being — not as denial, but as a genuine resource)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The Sun resonates with the Eastern recognition of luminosity — the warm, radiant quality of an unobscured mind, neither grasping nor dimmed by avoidance.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Sunstone, named for the sun across traditions, embodies The Sun's warmth, confidence, and the leadership that comes from genuine vitality.

cards.recommended_practice: name one genuine source of joy in your life right now, and tend it today with five minutes of real attention

best_overall.name: Sunstone
best_overall.reason: Sunstone is the radiant stone of joy, confidence, and warmth — The Sun in mineral form. Hold it when you want to reconnect with simple vitality; it supports the cultivated joy this card depicts, the kind that is noticed and tended rather than assumed.

best_upright.name: Citrine
best_upright.reason: Citrine carries the abundant, sunny energy of success and optimism. For an upright Sun draw, it supports the warmth of a season going well — and the generosity that genuine vitality makes possible.

best_reversed.name: Smoky Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When The Sun reverses into muted joy or ego inflation, Smoky Quartz grounds the over-bright energy back to earth. It is the stone for when confidence has crossed into overreach, or when joy needs steadying.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz deepens The Sun's love readings, where joy can be shared. It supports the simple warmth of two people enjoying each other, tending the good rather than taking it for granted.

best_daily_wear.name: Carnelian
best_daily_wear.reason: Carnelian is a daily stone of vitality, courage, and warm presence. Worn as jewelry, it keeps The Sun's radiance within reach through ordinary days — a reminder to bring genuine energy to what is in front of you.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'明性'luminosity(非执着)/ Sunstone 阳光之石
cards.source_notes: 四源共识=Sunstone(贴 Sun archetype)；best_reversed=Smoky Quartz(逆位 muted joy/ego 时 grounding)；合规：明示'joy is tended not assumed'+ 逆位非 failure 是临时 dimming

cards.name: Judgment
cards.theme: Reckoning, rebirth, hearing the call to a larger life
cards.upright_meaning: An angel blows a great trumpet in the sky, figures rise from their graves with arms outstretched, a white flag with a red cross. Judgment is the archetype of reckoning and renewal — the moment of honest self-assessment that precedes a rebirth. Upright, it speaks to hearing a call (to a larger life, a truer version of yourself), and to the clear-eyed reflection that lets you answer it.
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, Judgment often points to the shadow of self-assessment — harsh self-criticism instead of honest reckoning, refusing a call you have heard, or doubting the direction that is stirring. It is not a condemnation; it is an invitation to ask whether your inner voice has become a judge rather than a guide.
cards.psychological_lens: self-compassion vs self-criticism (the difference between honest reflection that frees you and an inner court that sentences you)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: Judgment resonates with the Eastern contemplative practice of honest self-examination without self-condemnation — seeing clearly, with compassion, so that reflection leads to renewal rather than guilt.
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Angelite, associated with gentle higher communication, embodies Judgment's call heard as guidance rather than as accusation.

cards.recommended_practice: reflect on one call or direction stirring in you, and write whether your inner response is guidance or self-judgment

best_overall.name: Angelite
best_overall.reason: Angelite is the stone of gentle higher communication and compassionate self-awareness — Judgment's call heard as guidance rather than accusation. Hold it while doing honest self-reflection; it supports the kind of reckoning that frees rather than sentences.

best_upright.name: Citrine
best_upright.reason: Citrine carries the warm, confident energy of renewal after honest assessment. For an upright Judgment draw, it supports the optimism needed to answer a call and step into a larger life.

best_reversed.name: Clear Quartz
best_reversed.reason: When Judgment reverses into self-criticism or doubt, Clear Quartz clears the noise of the inner court and clarifies the genuine signal beneath. It is the stone for telling guidance from condemnation.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz softens Judgment's love readings, where honest reckoning in a relationship must not become blame. It supports reflection that leads to renewal, not sentencing.

best_daily_wear.name: Selenite
best_daily_wear.reason: Selenite is a daily stone of clarity and cleansed perspective. Worn as jewelry (or kept close), it keeps Judgment's honest-reflection lens available — a reminder to see yourself with clarity and compassion together.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'如实自省非自责'/ Angelite 温柔的更高沟通
cards.source_notes: 矿物学裁决=Angelite(温柔更高沟通 最贴 Judgment 的'call heard as guidance'archetype，避免 condemnation 感)；best_reversed=Clear Quartz(逆位 self-criticism 时澄清信号)；合规：明示'reckoning 非谴责'

cards.name: The World
cards.theme: Completion, integration, wholeness — the end of a cycle
cards.upright_meaning: A figure dances within a large wreath of green, surrounded by the four fixed signs of the zodiac in the corners — lion, eagle, angel, bull. The World is the final card of the Major Arcana — the archetype of completion and integration, the end of a long cycle. Upright, it speaks to a sense of wholeness, to a chapter fulfilled, and quietly, to the recognition that every ending is also the threshold of the next beginning (back to The Fool).
cards.reversed_meaning: Reversed, The World often points to incompletion — loose ends, a delay of closure, the sense of being almost there but not quite. It is not a failure; it is an invitation to ask what one thing still needs finishing before this cycle can truly close, and the next begin.
cards.psychological_lens: self-actualization and integration (the movement toward wholeness — bringing together the parts of the self into a coherent whole)

eastern_anchors.tibetan: The World resonates with the Eastern recognition of completion as mandala — the integrated whole that, once seen, also reveals itself as the seed of the next cycle (the wreath that contains, and the dance that opens).
eastern_anchors.crystal_culture: Clear Quartz, the 'master healer' that harmonizes all energies, embodies The World's integration — the many brought into coherent wholeness.

cards.recommended_practice: name one cycle in your life that is reaching completion, and one loose end that still needs closing

best_overall.name: Clear Quartz
best_overall.reason: Clear Quartz is the master harmonizer — the stone that integrates many energies into coherent wholeness, just as The World integrates the lessons of the entire Major Arcana. Hold it while reflecting on a cycle reaching completion; it supports the clarity to honor what is whole and to recognize the threshold of what comes next.

best_upright.name: Selenite
best_upright.reason: Selenite is the stone of cleansed clarity and higher integration. For an upright World draw, it supports the lifted, clear state that recognizes wholeness when it arrives — the bright view from the summit of a completed climb.

best_reversed.name: Bloodstone
best_reversed.reason: When The World reverses into incompletion or loose ends, Bloodstone grounds the floating 'almost-there' energy back into practical action. It is the stone for the patient work of finishing what remains before the cycle can close.

best_love.name: Rose Quartz
best_love.reason: Rose Quartz deepens The World's love readings, where a relationship reaches a new level of integration. It supports honoring the wholeness two people have built, while staying open to its next chapter.

best_daily_wear.name: Lapis Lazuli
best_daily_wear.reason: Lapis Lazuli is a daily stone of wisdom, truth, and the inner royalty that comes from integration. Worn as jewelry, it keeps The World's sense of wholeness within reach — a reminder of how far you have come and what is now complete.

cards.eastern_lens: 东方'曼荼罗'圆满即下一环起点 / Clear Quartz 大师和合石
cards.source_notes: archetype裁决=Clear Quartz(master harmonizer 最贴 World 的'integration/wholeness'archetype)；best_reversed=Bloodstone(逆位 incompletion 时 grounding 回行动)；与 The Fool(0) 形成 Fool's Journey 闭环互链
